title: keeper-enterprise.v1
description: Offline schema body for Keeper Enterprise active users and role assignments. Other enterprise blocks remain empty scaffold stubs until future slices populate them.
$comment: TODO future slices per memo 2026-04-26_keeper-enterprise-v1-schema-design.md: nodes -> Decision 2; roles -> Decisions 3 and 5; teams -> Commander Surface Teams; enforcements -> Decision 3; aliases -> Decision 5 and Open Parent Calls; enterprise_pushes -> future slice after Commander surface audit.

x-keeper-live-proof.notes: memo: 2026-04-26_keeper-enterprise-v1-schema-design.md. Slice S covers active users and role_assignments only; nodes, roles, teams, enforcements, aliases, and enterprise_pushes are empty scaffold stubs pending future live-proof slices.

nodes.$comment: TODO future-slice per memo Decision 2: flat nodes[] with uid_ref, name, and parent_uid_ref.

roles.$comment: TODO future-slice per memo Decisions 3 and 5: roles[] owns role identity; assignments and enforcements stay split out.

teams.$comment: TODO future-slice per memo Commander Surface Teams row: teams[] owns team identity and restriction flags.

enforcements.$comment: TODO future-slice per memo Decision 3: role enforcement rows keyed by role reference and enforcement key.

aliases.$comment: TODO future-slice per memo Decision 5 and Open Parent Calls: user alias lifecycle after active-user proof.

enterprise_pushes.$comment: TODO future-slice after Commander enterprise push surface audit; not part of memo slice S live proof.

status.$comment: TODO future-slice: locked, disabled, and transferred are reserved until user lifecycle proof lands.

lock_status.$comment: TODO future-slice: locked states are reserved until lock lifecycle proof lands.

uid_ref.$comment: Optional row identity retained for semantic uid_ref uniqueness across populated slice-S blocks.
